headline: Edgar Morin: 'I doubt humanity even as I believe in it'
description: Over a series of conversations held between 2024 and 2026 with Le Monde, the philosopher and WWII resistant analyzed the contemporary world's upheaval, from the rise of authoritarianism in the West to conflicts in the Middle East, as well as the impact of new technologies and environmental issues.

headline: Trump cancels envoys' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks
Internet access in Iran becomes a privilege granted by the government
Bomb attack in Colombia leaves seven dead
description: The roadside bomb exploded in southwestern Colombia in a surge of violence blamed on FARC dissidents just weeks before the country's presidential election.

headline: Washington's resentment toward 'paper tiger' NATO is growing
description: The Trump administration has accused NATO allies of failing to show solidarity with the United States during the war with Iran. Its frustration is particularly strong toward Spain, France and the United Kingdom.

headline: 'Everything seemed to be cracking and melting': Former 'liquidator' recounts Chernobyl disaster, 40 years later
description: Halyna Kharshenko went to work at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant on April 26, 1986, unaware of the scale and severity of the explosion that had just occurred. The next day, she had to be rushed to a hospital unit where the staff would only approach her in full protective gear.

headline: Annette Messager: 'Without art, I would have become a very mean woman'
description: 'I wouldn't be where I am today if...' In this series, Le Monde interviews a public figure about a decisive moment in their life.  The internationally renowned visual artist recalls the strange atmosphere of the seaside town of Berck, where she grew up.

headline: 'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
Macron says EU joint defense clause is 'stronger' than NATO one
West Bank holds municipal elections under tight control of Palestinian Authority and Israel
NGOs call for a global response to US sanctions against ICC
Notorious burglar 'Le Chat' detained over Paris heists totaling €10 million
French teen gets 15-year jail term for killing teacher
French football's new anti-discrimination campaign is criticized
Five spots to bite into unforgettable madeleines
The gospel according to Pete Hegseth, the US 'secretary of war'
Driven by women and solo travelers, small group tours attract renewed interest
French hostages say they need more support after returning: 'Coming back was almost as hard as being detained. I felt isolated'
Hungary's Orban will not sit in parliament after election defeat
How Iranian propaganda lures the West into distraction
description: Pro-Iranian accounts are flooding the internet with AI-generated satirical videos that exploit Lego's popularity and Donald Trump's unpopularity, in an attempt to divert Western attention from the suffering of the Iranian people.

headline: 'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
description: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

headline: Sealing the internet: How Putin wants to cut Russia off from the rest of the world
description: Controlling information has become a matter of survival for the Russian leader. Since 2019, he has sought to create a 'sovereign RuNet' under his control and cut off from the world. The technical implementation of this project has accelerated since the start of the war in Ukraine in 2022.

headline: French Socialists' policy platform: Neither new nor complete, but at least it exists
'AI faces a growing backlash from the American public' due to its energy consumption and impact on jobs
'We cannot allow private companies, acting as both judge and party, to set the rules of the AI game'
